# Research We Fund

### About Our Research

PCORI funds patient-centered comparative clinical effectiveness research (CER) and CER-related projects. The key components of PCORI’s funded projects include patient-centered CER; [the science of engagement in research](https://www.pcori.org/resources/measuring-what-matters-advancing-science-and-practice-engagement); [dissemination and implementation of PCORI-funded CER results](https://www.pcori.org/implementation-evidence/putting-evidence-work); and investments to enhance the nation’s health [research infrastructure for conducting CER](https://www.pcori.org/about/about-pcori/research-infrastructure), including CER methods research. PCORI also funds projects that enhance communities' ability to partner on patient-centered CER, building communities equipped to engage in patient-centered CER through its Engagement Awards Program.

### Image

![A female African-American physician consults at the bedside of a male African-American patient who's lying down.](/sites/default/files/PCORI-Research-We-Support-Top-Banner-Image-867x350.jpg)

## Patient-Centered CER

PCORI funds patient-centered CER that looks at two or more different healthcare options to understand what works best for different people in different circumstances. PCORI’s goal is to fund patient-centered CER studies and research-related projects that provide reliable, useful information to help people make informed healthcare decisions and improve patient care and outcomes.

### Why CER?

With an ever-increasing number of effective care options for many conditions, Americans often face choices without information that can help them weigh the trade-offs of their options. PCORI funds research that addresses an important evidence gap or decisional dilemma faced by patients, families, clinicians and other health and healthcare community members.

### Elements of PCORI-Funded CER

PCORI funds patient-centered CER that advances the [National Priorities for Health](https://www.pcori.org/about/pcori-strategic-plan/pcori-strategic-plan-national-priorities-health) and is responsive to patient needs, outcomes and preferences. We offer both [broad and focused funding opportunities](https://www.pcori.org/funding-opportunities/what-who-we-fund/research-project-agenda-topic-themes-inform-focused-funding-opportunities) on topics identified directly by researchers and in response to specific health evidence needs informed by patients, caregivers and others from the broader healthcare community.

In PCORI-funded patient-centered CER, researchers compared two or more care approaches. Examples include:

Visit our [complete portfolio](/explore-our-portfolio) of funded studies and results.

PCORI-funded CER has the following characteristics:

In funding patient-centered CER studies, PCORI considers the following factors that could contribute to health outcomes for patient groups:

PCORI encourages awardees to capture, as appropriate [the full range of clinical and patient-centered outcomes](/research-related-projects/about-our-research/patient-centered-burdens-and-economic-outcomes/principles-consideration-full-range-outcomes-data-pcori-funded-research) relevant to patients, caregivers and stakeholders, including data on the potential burdens and economic impacts of the utilization of medical treatments, items and services.

Applicants for PCORI funding are encouraged but not obligated to consider use of PCORnet® to achieve their study goals. PCORnet provides a broad data infrastructure that supports conducting large observational studies that include clinical and patient-reported outcomes as well as large, pragmatic clinical trials embedded within participating systems.

## Strengthening CER and Expanding Its Impact

PCORI funds engagement in research, dissemination and implementation of research results and investments to enhance the nation’s health research infrastructure for conducting CER.

### Engagement in Research

The Eugene Washington PCORI Engagement Award Program supports bringing more patients, caregivers and the broader healthcare community into the research process. This program funds projects that provide resources to communities to equip them to better engage in patient-centered CER. These projects also help to disseminate PCORI-funded research results to those who need them.

### Dissemination and Implementation

PCORI prioritizes and funds the dissemination and implementation of its funded CER findings to ensure that new evidence quickly makes it into the hands of people who can use it to make informed choices about their health. PCORI's Dissemination and Implementation (D&I) Program funds projects that actively facilitate the uptake and integration of evidence from PCORI-funded studies into real-world clinical practice.

### CER Methodology Research

Improving existing CER methods benefits patients, caregivers and clinicians in making healthcare decisions. PCORI-funded methodology studies provide results that guide researchers in planning CER studies and also can help decision makers weigh the value of research results. More rigorous methods guide the design and implementation of CER that yields valid and actionable evidence. The [PCORI Methodology Standards](/research-related-projects/about-our-research/research-methodology/pcori-methodology-standards) and [PCORI Methodology Report](/research-related-projects/about-our-research/research-methodology/pcori-methodology-report) provide specific guidance on designing and conducting patient-centered CER.

### Systematic Reviews

PCORI funds systematic reviews that address high priority clinical questions for patients by summarizing and interpreting an array of findings reported by a broad range of key research studies on a given topic. Consistent with PCORI’s stakeholder-driven approach, these reviews incorporate patient and other stakeholder input throughout their development lifecycle. PCORI’s goal is to produce up-to-date, actionable evidence to inform important healthcare choices.

### Science of Engagement Research

Since its establishment, PCORI has accelerated the movement toward participatory research that seeks to involve patients, caregivers and many others as partners in the research process. However, little systematic evidence exists that documents which engagement methods are most effective. PCORI-funded [engagement in research studies](https://www.pcori.org/resources/measuring-what-matters-advancing-science-and-practice-engagement) seeks to generate evidence about engagement approaches in research, effective ways to measure meaningful engagement and more robust methods for conducting engaged research.  

![](/sites/default/files/PCORI-Research-We-Support-Infrasctructure-Banner-Graphic-867x260.jpg)

*Updated: April 18, 2025*

## What's Happening at PCORI?

The Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute sends weekly emails about opportunities to apply for funding, newly funded research studies and engagement projects, results of our funded research, webinars, and other new information posted on our site.

# Funding Opportunities

PCORI funds patient-centered comparative clinical effectiveness research (CER), issuing funding opportunity announcements several times each year. PCORI also funds projects that support CER, such as methodology studies, as well as projects that support engagement in research and dissemination and implementation of PCORI-funded research findings. Use the search/filters below to find available funding opportunities and take advantage of the following resources:

# PCORI announces new research opportunities to address critical health challenges

### About Us

### Image

![PCORI Funding Opportunities - Explore New Research Opportunities - Letters of Intent are due May 13, 2025. A male adults sits at a table, working on a laptop.](/sites/default/files/PCORI-New-C225-PFAs-April-2025-News-Release-Banner-1734x300.jpg)

WASHINGTON, D.C. — Every day, millions of Americans facing health challenges make decisions about their care without adequate information to understand the tradeoffs between available options. To help close these evidence gaps, the Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute (PCORI) is announcing new funding opportunities for patient-centered comparative clinical effectiveness research (CER) and related projects focused on a range of health concerns, including intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD), violence and trauma, maternal health and dementia.

Patient-centered CER compares two or more healthcare options to generate evidence about any differences in potential benefits or harms and gives patients and those who care for them information to make informed decisions that reflect their needs and preferences.

Among the four CER PCORI Funding Announcements (PFAs) opening today, two are focused on:

Additionally, PCORI is inviting proposals for any patient-centered CER topic through the following PFAs:

In addition to funding patient-centered CER to generate evidence that empowers patients, caregivers and other healthcare decision makers with information to make informed choices that reflect their needs and preferences, PCORI supports initiatives to accelerate the implementation of PCORI-funded CER results into clinical practice. Through its [Health Systems Implementation Initiative PFA](/funding-opportunities/announcement/pcori-call-proposals-hsii-implementation-projects-2025-hsii-participants-only), PCORI is inviting proposals for projects addressing hypertension, cancer treatment and mental health.

PCORI is also seeking proposals to [improve methods development](/funding-opportunities/announcement/improving-methods-conducting-patient-centered-comparative-clinical-effectiveness-research-pcori-funding-announcement-cycle-2-2025) for conducting patient-centered CER and the [science of engagement](/funding-opportunities/announcement/advancing-science-engagement-research-pcori-funding-announcement-cycle-2-2025) in patient-centered CER.

Details about the most recent funding announcements are on PCORI’s [website](/funding-opportunities).

### About PCORI

The Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute (PCORI) is a nonprofit organization authorized by Congress to fund patient-centered comparative clinical effectiveness research (CER). CER compares two or more healthcare options, generating evidence about any differences in potential benefits or harms to empower patients, caregivers and other healthcare decision makers with information to make informed choices that reflect their needs and preferences. PCORI emphasizes the engagement of patients, caregivers and the broader healthcare community in all aspects of PCORI-funded research and research-related activities, including the dissemination and uptake of research findings.

# PCORI commits to new patient-centered CER to empower health care decisions

### About Us

WASHINGTON, D.C. — Every day, millions of Americans make health care decisions without enough information to fully understand the trade-offs between approaches to care and make informed choices for themselves or their families.

### Research Funding Highlights

To help address these information gaps, the Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute (PCORI) has announced funding for new patient-centered comparative clinical effectiveness research (CER) studies across a range of conditions. These studies will help provide patients and caregivers with the evidence needed to make more informed health and health care decisions and more effectively manage their health.

The newly awarded patient-centered CER studies include research comparing:

Eight of the patient-centered CER studies will examine the long-term effects of treatments, interventions and programs previously studied in PCORI-funded CER, aiming to provide a more comprehensive understanding of the potential benefits and harms of these approaches.

Additional research awards support four studies to improve patient-centered CER methods and two studies to strengthen the evidence base on effectively engaging patients and other health and health care decisionmakers through the design and conduct of patient-centered CER.

### Image

![A collage of photos showing medical professionals of different genders and races interacting with patients of different genders and races.](/sites/default/files/PCORI-New-Funding-Awards-April-2025-News-Release-Banner-Collage-867x150.png)

### Accelerating uptake of PCORI-funded research results

PCORI also supports initiatives to accelerate the implementation of PCORI-funded CER results into clinical practice, addressing the often-cited 17-year gap between result publication and clinical practice adoption.

Through its [Health Systems Implementation Initiative (HSII)](/implementation-evidence/putting-evidence-work/health-systems-implementation-initiative), PCORI has announced 19 funding awards focused on:

### Image

![An infographic of showing where the 31 participants of PCORI's Health Systems Implementation Initiative are across the United States, based on whether they are implementing antibiotic prescribing, intensive lifestyle treatment for weight loss, addressing hypertension in adults, or monitoring ePROs during cancer treatment.](/sites/default/files/PCORI-New-Funding-Awards-April-2025-News-Release-HSII-Infographic-4800x3000.png)

With these new awards, HSII participant health systems have implementation projects across 31 states and the District of Columbia. These projects have the potential to impact care for 1.9 million patients across 3,800 care sites and include previously awarded PCORI-funded projects for intensive lifestyle treatment programs for weight loss as well as additional awards to improve antibiotic prescribing for children with ARTIs.

See the full list of new research and HSII awards on PCORI’s [website](/explore-our-portfolio).

All award funding has been approved pending final PCORI contractual considerations.

### About PCORI

The Patient-Centered Outcomes Research Institute (PCORI) is a nonprofit organization authorized by Congress to fund patient-centered comparative clinical effectiveness research (CER). CER compares two or more healthcare options, generating evidence about any differences in potential benefits or harms to empower patients, caregivers and other healthcare decision makers with information to make informed choices that reflect their needs and preferences. PCORI emphasizes the engagement of patients, caregivers and the broader healthcare community in all aspects of PCORI-funded research and research-related activities, including the dissemination and uptake of research findings.

enriched_points.expanded_content: PCORI allocates substantial funding for patient-centered CER, enabling research that compares healthcare options and addresses critical health challenges, empowering informed patient decisions.
PCORI emphasizes collaboration with patients, caregivers, and healthcare professionals, ensuring their involvement in research processes to reflect real-world needs and preferences.
PCORI prioritizes swift dissemination of research results, integrating findings into clinical practice through initiatives that bridge the gap between research and healthcare implementation.
